_doc: Hint packs for The Heist classroom game. Each pack is a closed pool of words students pick passwords from AND a stream of 'earn' questions for the left-side panel. Each entry needs: word (the vault password / answer), def (used as one of the 4 MCQ options for the earn panel), example (optional, used for fill-in earn variant), tabooHint (the Taboo-style clue an attacker sees when targeting a vault — must EVOKE the word without naming it or using its forms). Add new packs by appending to the `packs` array.

packs.name: B1 Common Verbs
packs.description: Twenty everyday verbs from B1 vocabulary.

words.def: to suffer something painful for a long time
words.tabooHint: What a marathon runner does for hours.

words.def: to say you are sorry
words.example: I want to ______ for being late.
words.tabooHint: What you say after bumping into a stranger.

words.def: to become healthy again after illness
words.example: It took her weeks to ______ from the flu.
words.tabooHint: What you slowly do after the flu.

words.def: to convince someone to do something
words.tabooHint: What a good salesperson does to you.

words.def: to use someone unfairly for your own benefit
words.tabooHint: What a cruel boss does to workers.

words.def: to refuse to give something
words.example: They will ______ the salary if you break the rules.
words.tabooHint: What a parent does with dessert when you misbehave.

words.def: to put money into something to make more
words.tabooHint: What you do with savings hoping they grow.

words.def: to receive money or things from someone who has died
words.example: He will ______ the house from his grandfather.
words.tabooHint: What happens when a rich uncle dies.

words.def: to have enough money to pay for something
words.tabooHint: What you say you can't do when something is too expensive.

words.def: to succeed in doing something difficult
words.tabooHint: What an Olympic athlete finally does after years of training.

words.def: to find out or decide something officially
words.tabooHint: What a judge does after hearing all the evidence.

words.def: to promise to do something seriously
words.example: Are you ready to ______ to this project?
words.tabooHint: What you do at the altar when you say 'I do'.

words.def: to give something to a charity or person who needs it
words.tabooHint: What you do with old clothes at a charity bin.

words.def: to say you are not happy about something
words.tabooHint: What an unhappy customer does at the restaurant.

words.def: to feel grateful for something
words.tabooHint: What you feel after a friend helps you move.

words.def: to think something is true without proof
words.example: Don't ______ anything until you have evidence.
words.tabooHint: What you do when you guess without checking.

words.def: to admit you did something wrong
words.tabooHint: What a guilty criminal finally does at the police station.

words.def: to rely on someone or something
words.tabooHint: What a baby does on its parents for everything.

words.def: to discuss in order to reach an agreement
words.example: They will ______ a new contract tomorrow.
words.tabooHint: What two business people do over the price of a deal.

words.def: to find something for the first time
words.tabooHint: What Columbus did in 1492.

packs.name: A2 Common Verbs
packs.description: Twenty everyday verbs from A2 vocabulary — gentler than the B1 pack.

words.def: to reach a place after a journey
words.example: We will ______ at the airport at 9 a.m.
words.tabooHint: What the plane does at the airport.

words.def: to take something for a short time, planning to give it back
words.tabooHint: What you ask a friend to do with their pen for ten minutes.

words.def: to think that something is true
words.tabooHint: What you do when someone says a ghost is real.

words.def: to pick one from two or more options
words.tabooHint: What you do at a restaurant when there are too many menu items.

words.def: to prepare food using heat
words.tabooHint: What a chef does for a living.

words.def: to like doing something
words.tabooHint: What you do at your own birthday party.

words.def: to make something clear by giving more details
words.tabooHint: What a teacher does when the class looks confused.

words.def: to fail to remember something
words.tabooHint: What you wish you didn't do before an exam.

words.def: (of an event) to take place
words.tabooHint: What people ask about when they hear an ambulance siren.

words.def: to have something as a part
words.tabooHint: What a tip does to your total restaurant bill.

words.def: to ask someone to come to an event
words.example: She ______ d 50 people to the party.
words.tabooHint: What you do to friends for your wedding.

words.def: to go away from a place
words.tabooHint: What you do when the party ends.

words.def: to see or become aware of something
words.tabooHint: What you do to a friend's new haircut.

words.def: to get something ready
words.tabooHint: What a chef does in the kitchen before customers arrive.

words.def: to keep something in your mind
words.tabooHint: What you struggle to do with all your passwords.

words.def: to give part of something to someone else
words.tabooHint: What you do with a pizza when friends visit.

words.def: to go from one place to another
words.tabooHint: What you do to escape the cold winter.

words.def: to feel anxious about something
words.tabooHint: What parents do when their teenager is late home.

words.def: to reply to a question
words.tabooHint: What you do when the phone rings.

words.def: to say strongly that you will do something
words.tabooHint: What a child does to their mother to get dessert.

packs.name: B2 Advanced Verbs
packs.description: Twenty higher-frequency B2 verbs students often see in IELTS / academic writing.

words.def: to claim something is true without proof
words.example: The newspaper ______ d he took bribes.
words.tabooHint: What a tabloid does when it has no real evidence.

words.def: to make something wider or more varied
words.tabooHint: What backpacking around Asia does to your worldview.

words.def: to combine several things into one stronger whole
words.tabooHint: What a bank does to many small debts when you refinance.

words.def: to become worse
words.tabooHint: What fruit does when you leave it on the counter for a week.

words.def: to explain something in more detail
words.tabooHint: What a professor does when the class looks confused.

words.def: to change frequently in level or amount
words.tabooHint: What stock prices do all day on the screen.

words.def: to understand something complex
words.tabooHint: What you can barely do with a slippery concept on the first try.

words.def: to delay or make something difficult
words.tabooHint: What slow internet does to your work-from-home day.

words.def: to put a plan or policy into action
words.example: The school will ______ the new rules.
words.tabooHint: What a manager does with a new company policy.

words.def: to give a good reason for something
words.tabooHint: What you try to do for an expensive impulse buy when your partner asks.

words.def: to stay around longer than expected
words.tabooHint: What perfume does in an elevator long after the wearer leaves.

words.def: to make something less harmful or severe
words.tabooHint: What sunscreen does to UV damage.

words.def: to care for and encourage growth
words.tabooHint: What a gardener does to a delicate seedling every morning.

words.def: to repair or improve something thoroughly
words.tabooHint: What a mechanic does to a broken-down car over several days.

words.def: to become aware of something through the senses
words.tabooHint: What a dog does that humans completely miss.

words.def: to satisfy a thirst or strong desire
words.tabooHint: What ice water does after a long run.

words.def: to look like or be similar to
words.tabooHint: What a baby tends to do to one of their parents.

words.def: to examine carefully and in detail
words.tabooHint: What a detective does to every piece of evidence under a magnifier.

words.def: to accept something unpleasant without complaining
words.tabooHint: What you can barely do with a noisy neighbour at 2am.

words.def: to weaken something gradually
words.tabooHint: What constant gossip does to a friendship over time.
